name: ice beast
shortDescription: An ice beast token or trophy claim tied to a creature of frost, heavy fur, and winter patience. Hunters sell it with stories of cracked breath and frozen tracks, while buyers ask whether the cold still clings because they enjoy being uneasy.
flavorText: “Some beasts leave footprints. This one leaves weather.”

name: kobold mage
shortDescription: A kobold mage writ, charm bundle, or bounty mark associated with tunnel sorcery and troublesome little rituals. Mine owners and dungeon scouts pay attention to such records, because one clever spellcaster can make a narrow passage cost far more than stone.
flavorText: “Never laugh at a small mage in a small tunnel.”

name: kobold
shortDescription: A kobold token or capture note from the busy, sharp-eyed folk of caves, mines, and forgotten works. Individually modest, they still matter to traders because kobolds mean traps, tools, salvage, stolen goods, and negotiations conducted at ankle height.
flavorText: “Kobolds are cheap trouble until they arrive in numbers.”

name: fabled lava worm
shortDescription: A fabled lava worm claim from molten tunnels where ordinary maps become polite fiction. Its mention draws smiths, fire priests, monster scholars, and reckless collectors, all eager for proof that something alive can move through heat like a road.
flavorText: “The worm does not burrow through earth. It remembers when earth was fire.”

name: scarce lindwurm
shortDescription: A scarce lindwurm specimen claim or scale-right tied to a long, dangerous drake of old hills and bad roads. It carries value for alchemists, armorers, and trophy houses, though every contract should state whether the tail was truly counted.
flavorText: “Long beasts make long stories and longer invoices.”

name: mutant beast
shortDescription: A mutant beast record, bounty proof, or preserved sample from a creature changed by bad magic, strange diet, or places best left unnamed. Scholars call it useful evidence; villagers call it proof the old well should have been sealed sooner.
flavorText: “Mutation is nature arguing with a bad idea.”

name: mutant rat
shortDescription: A mutant rat token or specimen, unpleasantly valuable to plague doctors, sewer guilds, and alchemists studying what filth can become when helped by magic. It is small stock, but no buyer handles it without asking about the cage first.
flavorText: “A rat is a nuisance. A changed rat is a report.”

name: raiju
shortDescription: A raiju token linked to a lightning-born creature spoken of in storm country with respect and nervous repair bills. Collectors prize the name, mages prize the charge, and stable owners simply ask that it be kept away from horses.
flavorText: “Thunder runs faster when it has paws.”

name: rock worm
shortDescription: A rock worm claim from quarries, dry ravines, and deep roads where stone sometimes moves for reasons no mason approves of. Miners buy warnings, alchemists buy teeth and grit, and caravan masters buy any knowledge that keeps wheels above ground.
flavorText: “The mountain has smaller mouths than people think.”

name: salamander
shortDescription: A salamander token or specimen tied to fire-warmed stones, forge vents, and old volcanic hollows. Its hide, ash, or service writ interests smiths and elementalists, though traders store the paperwork separately from lamp oil out of habit and fear.
flavorText: “A salamander treats embers like bathwater.”

name: salamander mystic
shortDescription: A salamander mystic record connected to fire rites, ember prayers, and the kind of wisdom that smells faintly of smoke. Temples and scholars may bid for it, but only careful brokers ask whether the mystic consented to being catalogued.
flavorText: “Not every flame burns; some interpret.”

name: salamander stormcaller
shortDescription: A salamander stormcaller token, rare enough to make elemental circles argue over whether thunder can truly serve flame. It is dangerous prestige stock, desired by war mages and weather cults, and best transported during calm skies and quiet tempers.
flavorText: “When fire calls the storm, sensible roofs pray first.”

name: salamander warrior
shortDescription: A salamander warrior writ or trophy note tied to disciplined fighters from hot stone regions and ember-lit strongholds. Mercenary brokers respect the reputation, while armorers ask practical questions about heat, grips, and what exactly survived the encounter.
flavorText: “A warrior of flame does not need a banner to be noticed.”

name: scarce sphinx
shortDescription: A scarce sphinx token or encounter proof from desert ruins where riddles can cost more than tolls. Scholars, collectors, and temple courts prize such records, though experienced merchants never advertise a sphinx item without expecting difficult questions.
flavorText: “A sphinx makes buyers prove they deserve the price.”

name: spriggan berserker
shortDescription: A spriggan berserker writ tied to wild forest fighters whose fury is remembered in broken shields and trampled camps. Border lords may buy the service, scholars may study the customs, and lumber merchants usually prefer pretending they never heard the name.
flavorText: “The forest has warriors for when warnings fail.”

name: spriggan defender
shortDescription: A spriggan defender token associated with guarded groves, hidden paths, and patient woodland resistance. It has value to diplomats, wardens, and anyone trying to cross living forest without becoming a lesson carved into bark.
flavorText: “A defender of roots measures time differently from invaders.”

name: spriggan enchanter
shortDescription: A spriggan enchanter record tied to forest magic, old songs, and the subtle art of making travelers doubt their own direction. Courts and mage guilds value it, but seasoned traders keep such tokens wrapped in iron-thread cloth.
flavorText: “The charm begins when the path looks familiar twice.”

name: spriggan rider
shortDescription: A spriggan rider writ from forest warbands that move through brush faster than roads can explain. Scouts, nobles, and collectors all want proof of such cavalry, though stablemasters complain there is never a normal mount involved.
flavorText: “In deep woods, hooves are optional; speed is not.”

name: market swamp drake
shortDescription: A market-certified swamp drake claim, processed for trade rather than legend, with enough paperwork to comfort cautious buyers. Its hide, teeth, or capture rights suit armorers and apothecaries, while marsh guides quietly charge extra for pointing out where it came from.
flavorText: “The swamp sells its monsters damp and overpriced.”

name: tengu
shortDescription: A tengu token or encounter writ tied to sharp-beaked folk of high perches, windy passes, and quick bargains. Messengers, duelists, and collectors value the association, especially when the item comes with proof that the seller was not simply mocked by a crow.
flavorText: “A tengu looks down on most markets because it arrives above them.”

name: tengu conjurer
shortDescription: A tengu conjurer record associated with airy tricks, sudden gusts, and magic performed from places archers dislike. Mage guilds buy such notes for study, while caravan guards buy them as warnings against laughing at feathers during negotiations.
flavorText: “The spell is cast before the insult lands.”

name: tengu reaver
shortDescription: A tengu reaver writ or trophy mark from a fast raider of cliffs and exposed roads. Its value lies in reputation as much as material; merchants pay attention to anything that explains why a guarded caravan arrived lighter than expected.
flavorText: “Reavers prefer the sky because roads have witnesses.”

name: tengu warrior
shortDescription: A tengu warrior token linked to disciplined fighters who favor height, speed, and blades that arrive from unkind angles. Mercenary buyers respect the skill, though quartermasters grumble that perch rights are never listed in standard lodging costs.
flavorText: “A ground fight is fair. A tengu rarely insists on fair.”

name: treant
shortDescription: A treant record, seed-token, or elderwood claim tied to the walking guardians of ancient forests. It draws druids, nobles, and timber guild spies in equal measure, because a living tree that can object changes the price of every axe nearby.
flavorText: “Old trees do not hurry. They have watched hurry die.”

name: vine stalker
shortDescription: A vine stalker token from tangled wetlands and deep green ruins where plants learn habits better left to beasts. Garden alchemists and wardens study it carefully, while ordinary buyers mostly ask whether the sample is still growing.
flavorText: “The vine did not chase. It merely arrived where you were going.”

name: war dog
shortDescription: A war dog writ, collar token, or trained-hound contract for a disciplined animal raised to guard camps, caravans, and nervous doors. Buyers judge breed, obedience, and handler notes, because courage without training is just a bill with teeth.
flavorText: “Loyalty is valuable. Loyalty that bites on command is expensive.”

name: dusk wolf
shortDescription: A dusk wolf token or pelt claim from shadowed woods where the light fails early and howls travel far. Hunters sell the story with care, and wardens buy the warning, since dusk wolves make even familiar paths feel borrowed.
flavorText: “Some wolves wait for night. Dusk wolves bring enough of it with them.”

name: yaktaur
shortDescription: A yaktaur token from high cold country, linked to horned strength, mountain stubbornness, and difficult passes. Traders value such records for diplomacy and military contracts, while lowland buyers mostly underestimate how much mountain folk dislike being underestimated.
flavorText: “The mountain gave it hooves, horns, and no patience for lowland jokes.”

name: notable death drake
shortDescription: A notable death drake claim with enough reputation to make collectors lean in and stable animals lean away. Alchemists prize the remains, chroniclers prize the tale, and sensible merchants price it according to danger, proof, and how fresh the fear still feels.
flavorText: “Some drakes die into stories. This one made stories die first.”

name: death knight
shortDescription: A death knight token, relic mark, or campaign proof tied to an armored figure whose legend walks beyond ordinary mortality. Temples, war colleges, and collectors of grim history all bid carefully, because even a token of such a knight feels like unfinished business.
flavorText: “Armor remembers the oath after the wearer forgets breath.”

name: small death knight
shortDescription: A small death knight figure or lesser field token, unsettling despite its reduced scale. It is bought by tacticians, necromantic scholars, and collectors who prefer danger in manageable dimensions, though nobody calls it harmless more than once.
flavorText: “Small only describes the silhouette, not the omen.”

name: demonic plant
shortDescription: A demonic plant specimen, seed claim, or burned-root bundle from soil that should probably be left unwatered. Apothecaries, exorcists, and reckless gardeners all find uses for it, but merchants keep it away from window light and encouragement.
flavorText: “Some weeds are pulled. Others are negotiated with by priests.”

name: ash fiend spawn
shortDescription: An ash fiend spawn token associated with soot, bitter heat, and creatures that seem born from the remains of worse fires. It has value to demonologists and ward-makers, though warehouses charge extra when stock smells like a burned warning.
flavorText: “Ash is what the flame leaves behind when it wants heirs.”

name: dryad
shortDescription: A dryad token or grove record tied to the living spirit of old trees and carefully guarded woodland places. Poets romanticize it, diplomats fear mishandling it, and merchants learn quickly that forest gifts are never just merchandise.
flavorText: “A dryad's favor cannot be nailed to a receipt.”

name: enchantress
shortDescription: An enchantress writ, portrait-token, or court record associated with magic, influence, and conversations that end differently than they began. Courts and guilds pay well for such connections, though wise brokers never assume charm is the same as permission.
flavorText: “The finest enchantments leave the buyer thinking the idea was theirs.”

name: powerful weaver
shortDescription: A powerful weaver record tied to threads, fate-work, spell-cloth, or craft so precise it borders on prophecy. Tailors admire the skill, mages study the pattern, and merchants charge more when nobody can agree whether the weaving is art or warning.
flavorText: “Some hands make cloth. Some make consequences with a hem.”

name: grand avatar
shortDescription: A grand avatar token, effigy, or encounter claim linked to a mighty embodied force rather than a simple creature. Temples, scholars, and ambitious rulers all value it, though ownership is mostly a legal fiction wrapped around awe.
flavorText: “When power takes a shape, merchants invent a category.”

name: hell knight
shortDescription: A hell knight token or grim campaign mark tied to armored servants of infernal war. It is valuable to collectors and wardens, but even jaded brokers handle such stock with gloves, iron seals, and fewer jokes than usual.
flavorText: “Some armor is forged to protect. Some is forged to announce damnation.”

name: orb guardian
shortDescription: An orb guardian token linked to round-eyed wardens, floating sentries, or constructs that watch too much and forgive too little. Mages and vault-keepers prize the concept, while thieves hate any merchandise that makes staring a profession.
flavorText: “The guardian does not blink because the vault cannot afford it.”

name: orb guardian infant
shortDescription: An orb guardian infant token, smaller and stranger than the full watcher, often collected for study before its instincts become expensive. Apprentices call it charming, masters call it a containment matter, and merchants call it fragile premium stock.
flavorText: “Even a small eye can learn where secrets are kept.”

name: shadow imp
shortDescription: A shadow imp token or capture note from the small, sly kind of darkness that steals attention before it steals anything else. Demon brokers and ward-makers value it, while innkeepers mostly want assurance that it is not under the counter.
flavorText: “It is only a little shadow until it starts laughing.”

name: scarce blue dragon hide
shortDescription: A scarce blue dragon hide, prized for its color, resilience, and the storm-born prestige attached to anything that once covered a dragon. Armorers pay dearly, collectors pay foolishly, and every buyer wants proof it is not dyed cowhide with confidence.
flavorText: “Blue dragon hide holds the memory of thunder under the skin.”

name: fabled gold dragon hide
shortDescription: A fabled gold dragon hide of legendary rarity, radiant enough in reputation that the material becomes political before it becomes armor. Kings, temples, and master crafters may all bid, though any seller should expect verification, suspicion, and bodyguards.
flavorText: “Gold dragon hide is not bought. It is survived into ownership.”

name: scarce ice dragon hide
shortDescription: A scarce ice dragon hide, pale and cold in legend, sought by armorers who work for northern courts and mages who think frost can be stitched into protection. It travels best wrapped dry, warded well, and priced without apology.
flavorText: “Winter leaves scales when it learns to fly.”

name: scarce Glassmere dragon hide
shortDescription: A scarce Glassmere dragon hide tied to the strange shimmer of mirror-water country and the beasts said to haunt its bright marshes. Its value depends on provenance, luster, and whether the buyer trusts reflections more than witnesses.
flavorText: “Glassmere hides shine as if the dragon never stopped watching.”

name: fabled pearl dragon hide
shortDescription: A fabled pearl dragon hide, luminous and rare enough to make armorers speak gently around it. Coastal courts, temple treasuries, and collectors of impossible materials all desire it, because beauty that once belonged to a dragon carries its own authority.
flavorText: “Pearl dragon hide glows like moonlight taught to resist blades.”

name: fabled quicksilver dragon hide
shortDescription: A fabled quicksilver dragon hide, prized for shifting sheen, strange flexibility, and rumors that it never rests in the same color twice. It is nightmare stock for appraisers and dream stock for master crafters, which makes the price wonderfully unreasonable.
flavorText: “Quicksilver hide moves in the eye before it moves in the hand.”

name: fabled shadow dragon hide
shortDescription: A fabled shadow dragon hide, dark enough in legend to drink candlelight along its folded edges. Assassins, nobles, and warded vaults all have reasons to want it, though every transaction feels quieter than the coin involved deserves.
flavorText: “Shadow dragon hide is what darkness wore when it needed armor.”

name: fabled silver dragon hide
shortDescription: A fabled silver dragon hide, bright, noble, and wrapped in enough old stories to raise any auction room's posture. It tempts royal armorers and relic collectors alike, especially when the surface catches light like a blade deciding to become cloth.
flavorText: “Silver dragon hide makes moonlight look practical.”

name: notable swamp dragon hide
shortDescription: A notable swamp dragon hide, heavier with mud-country reputation than with beauty, but valued for toughness and rare origin. Armorers like its resilience, marsh lords like the story, and haulers like it best once the smell has faded.
flavorText: “Swamp dragon hide proves even muck can grow scales worth selling.”
